wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this project overview section focused on the qoder full-stack demo application. Explain its purpose as a modern Vue 3 + Spring Boot 3 integration example, architecture overview, and relationship between frontend and backend components. Document the implementation approach, technology stack choices, and usage patterns. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase. Provide practical examples demonstrating the complete development workflow from backend API creation to frontend consumption. Document the project structure, key components, and integration patterns. Include diagrams where appropriate to illustrate the overall system architecture and data flow between frontend and backend.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Spring Boot application architecture section. Explain the project structure, main application entry point, and Spring Boot auto-configuration features. Document the @SpringBootApplication annotation, component scanning, and application startup process. Cover the Maven build configuration, dependency management, and project organization. Include detailed explanations of the application class structure, package organization, and architectural patterns used. Provide practical examples of extending the application structure and explain best practices for Spring Boot project organization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Vue 3 application architecture section. Explain the component-based architecture design, project structure organization, and Vue 3 Composition API usage patterns. Document the root application component structure, template organization, and script setup implementation. Cover the main.ts entry point configuration, global imports, and application initialization process. Include detailed explanations of the component hierarchy, scoped styling approach, and responsive design patterns. Provide practical examples of component composition, prop passing, and event handling. Address the integration with Vue Router and the overall application flow from entry point to rendered components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the qoder full-stack demo application. Include prerequisites (Java 17, Node.js), step-by-step installation instructions for both backend and frontend components, and quick start guide for running the complete application. Document development environment setup, dependency installation, and initial configuration. Provide clear instructions for starting the Spring Boot backend server and Vue 3 frontend development server. Include verification steps to ensure proper setup and explain the expected behavior when accessing the application. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for beginners.

wiki_catalogs.name: REST API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the REST API endpoints section. Document the /api/hello endpoint including HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and response formats. Explain the controller implementation, response object structure with message and timestamp fields, and error handling strategies. Include practical examples of API consumption, curl commands for testing, and integration patterns. Document the application.yml configuration for server settings and port configuration. Provide guidelines for extending the API with additional endpoints and explain RESTful design principles applied in this project.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Vue Router implementation section. Thoroughly explain the routing configuration, route definitions, and navigation patterns. Document the router index configuration, route guards, and lazy loading strategies. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing route setup, navigation links, and RouterView usage. Explain the relationship between routes and view components, parameter passing, and query handling. Address common routing scenarios, nested routes, and programmatic navigation. Provide guidelines for extending the routing system with new pages and maintaining clean navigation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Application (Spring Boot)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Spring Boot backend application section. Explain the application architecture, project structure, and main entry point configuration. Document the REST API endpoint implementation, controller patterns, and response handling. Cover application properties configuration, development tools setup, and Maven build configuration. Include detailed explanations of the HelloController implementation, HTTP endpoint specifications, and response formats. Document the Spring Boot application startup process, auto-configuration features, and development workflow with DevTools. Provide practical examples of extending the backend with additional endpoints and explain best practices for Spring Boot development.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Spring Boot development tools section. Document the application.yml configuration including server settings, logging configuration, and development-specific properties. Explain the Maven POM configuration, dependency management, and plugin setup. Cover Spring Boot DevTools configuration for hot reloading, automatic restarts, and development workflow optimization. Include practical examples of configuring application properties, setting up development environments, and troubleshooting common development issues. Document best practices for Spring Boot development and deployment configurations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Vue 3 component development patterns. Describe the component structure, Composition API usage, and reusability principles. Document the HelloWorld component implementation, icon components organization, and welcome components functionality. Include usage examples with code snippets, prop definitions, and event handling patterns. Provide guidelines for component composition, slot usage, and state management within components. Address component styling, scoped CSS, and responsive design implementation. Document best practices for component naming, folder organization, and maintainable component architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Application (Vue 3)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Vue 3 frontend application section. Explain the component-based architecture, project structure, and Vue 3 Composition API usage patterns. Document the routing system implementation, state management approach, and API integration patterns. Cover the API client configuration, HTTP request handling, and error management strategies. Include detailed explanations of the component hierarchy, navigation system, and development workflow with Vite. Document the integration between frontend and backend through API calls, proxy configuration, and real-time communication patterns. Provide practical examples of extending the frontend with additional components and explain best practices for Vue 3 development.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API integration documentation for the frontend-backend communication. Document the centralized API client configuration, HTTP request patterns, and response handling strategies. Explain the Axios client setup, base URL configuration, and request/response interceptors. Include detailed examples of API calls from components, state management integration, and error handling patterns. Document the API demo view implementation, loading states, and user feedback mechanisms. Address authentication patterns, request cancellation, and retry strategies. Provide guidelines for extending API functionality and integrating with additional backend services.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Documentation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the RESTful endpoints in the qoder application. Document the HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and error handling strategies for the /api/hello endpoint. Include detailed parameter specifications, response format examples, and status codes. Explain the API client implementation in the frontend, including axios configuration, request/response handling, and error management. Document the integration patterns between frontend and backend, proxy configuration for development, and cross-origin resource sharing setup. Provide practical examples of API consumption, common use cases, and client implementation guidelines. Address debugging approaches, testing strategies, and performance considerations for API communication.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Environment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Vite development environment configuration. Thoroughly explain the Vite configuration setup, proxy configuration for backend integration, and development server options. Document the package.json scripts, dependency management, and build process configuration. Include concrete examples of development workflow, hot reloading setup, and environment variable handling. Explain TypeScript configuration, ESLint setup, and testing framework integration. Address performance optimization, asset handling, and development best practices. Provide guidelines for customizing the development environment and extending build configurations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the development workflow section focused on the integrated development experience of the full-stack application. Explain the development environment setup, hot reloading capabilities, and proxy configuration for seamless frontend-backend communication. Document the build processes for both frontend and backend, development server configurations, and cross-origin resource sharing setup. Cover the development workflow patterns, debugging approaches, and testing strategies. Include practical examples of common development scenarios, troubleshooting guides for development environment issues, and best practices for efficient development. Address the coordination between frontend and backend development, shared development tools, and collaborative development workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the testing strategy section focused on quality assurance approaches for the full-stack application. Document the backend testing framework setup, unit testing patterns, and integration testing strategies using JUnit 5. Explain the frontend testing configuration, testing libraries setup, and testing workflow for Vue 3 components. Cover test execution commands, testing best practices, and continuous integration considerations. Include practical examples of testing API endpoints, frontend components, and integration tests. Address testing challenges specific to full-stack applications, mock strategies for external dependencies, and automated testing approaches. Provide guidance on test coverage, performance testing, and debugging test failures.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Vue3 + Spring Boot 3 Full Stack Demo Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
This project serves as a comprehensive demonstration of modern full-stack web development using Vue 3 (frontend) and Spring Boot 3 (backend). It showcases best practices for building responsive web applications with TypeScript, reactive state management, and RESTful API integration.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Demonstrate seamless frontend-backend integration using Vite and Spring Boot DevTools
- Implement modern Vue 3 composition API patterns with TypeScript
- Showcase RESTful API communication between Vue 3 components and Spring Boot controllers
- Provide a scalable foundation for enterprise web applications
- Illustrate proper development workflow with hot reloading and proxy configuration

### Target Audience
- Frontend developers learning Vue 3 ecosystem
- Backend developers exploring Spring Boot 3 features
- Full-stack developers seeking modern development patterns
- Teams building microservices with React/Vue frontends

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Vue 3 Frontend] --> B[Vite Development Server]
    B --> C[API Proxy /api]
    C --> D[Spring Boot Backend]
    D --> E[REST Controller]
    E --> F[JSON Response]
    
    G[Vue Router] --> H[Component Views]
    H --> I[ApiDemoView]
    I --> J[API Client]
    J --> K[Axios Instance]
```

### Design Patterns

**Frontend Architecture:**
- **Composition API Pattern**: Vue 3 script setup with reactive state management
- **API Client Pattern**: Centralized HTTP client configuration with TypeScript interfaces
- **Component-Based Architecture**: Modular view components with scoped styling
- **Proxy Pattern**: Vite proxy configuration for seamless API communication

**Backend Architecture:**
- **RESTful Controller Pattern**: Spring Boot @RestController with proper HTTP responses
- **Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS)**: Configured for frontend integration
- **Development Tools Integration**: Spring Boot DevTools for hot reloading

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Browser
    participant Vite
    participant Proxy
    participant SpringBoot
    participant Controller
    
    Browser->>+Vite: GET /api-demo
    Vite->>+Proxy: /api/hello
    Proxy->>+SpringBoot: Forward request
    SpringBoot->>+Controller: Handle request
    Controller->>+SpringBoot: Return JSON
    SpringBoot-->>-Proxy: Response
    Proxy-->>-Vite: API Response
    Vite-->>-Browser: Rendered View
```

### Data Flow Diagrams

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Action] --> B[Vue Component]
    B --> C[API Call]
    C --> D[Axios Client]
    D --> E[Proxy Configuration]
    E --> F[Spring Boot Controller]
    F --> G[Response Object]
    G --> H[Component State Update]
    H --> I[UI Rendering]
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Entry Point:**
- `springboot3-demo/src/main/java/com/example/demo/DemoApplication.java` - Spring Boot application startup

**Frontend Entry Point:**
- `vue3-springboot-demo/src/main.ts` - Vue 3 application initialization

### Core Modules

**Backend Controllers:**
- `springboot3-demo/src/main/java/com/example/demo/controller/HelloController.java` - REST endpoint implementation

**Frontend Components:**
- `vue3-springboot-demo/src/views/ApiDemoView.vue` - API demonstration component
- `vue3-springboot-demo/src/App.vue` - Root application component

### Configuration Approach

**Backend Configuration:**
- `springboot3-demo/src/main/resources/application.yml` - Server settings and logging configuration

**Frontend Configuration:**
- `vue3-springboot-demo/vite.config.ts` - Development server and proxy configuration
- `vue3-springboot-demo/package.json` - Dependencies and build scripts

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies:**
- Spring Boot Starter Web for REST API development
- Spring Boot DevTools for development efficiency
- Lombok for reducing boilerplate code
- JUnit 5 for testing framework

**Frontend Dependencies:**
- Vue 3.5.32 with Composition API support
- Vue Router 5.0.4 for client-side routing
- Pinia 3.0.4 for state management
- Axios 1.15.1 for HTTP client functionality
- TypeScript 6.0.0 for type safety

### Integration Points

**API Communication:**
- `vue3-springboot-demo/src/api/index.ts` - Centralized API client configuration
- Cross-origin configuration in backend controller for frontend integration

**Development Workflow:**
- Vite proxy configuration for seamless API communication during development
- Spring Boot DevTools for automatic application restarts

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[App.vue] --> B[Router Navigation]
    B --> C[HomeView]
    B --> D[AboutView]
    B --> E[ApiDemoView]
    
    E --> F[HelloWorld Component]
    E --> G[API Client]
    G --> H[Spring Boot Controller]
    
    I[Vue Router] --> J[Route Configuration]
    J --> E
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Frontend Features:**
- Reactive state management with Vue 3 Composition API
- Type-safe API communication with TypeScript interfaces
- Component-based architecture with modular design
- Responsive UI with scoped CSS styling
- Dynamic content loading with error handling

**Backend Features:**
- RESTful API endpoints with proper HTTP responses
- Cross-origin resource sharing configuration
- Development-friendly tools and hot reloading
- Structured logging and debugging capabilities

### Implementation Highlights

**API Integration:**
- `vue3-springboot-demo/src/api/index.ts` - Centralized API client with timeout and headers configuration
- `vue3-springboot-demo/src/views/ApiDemoView.vue` - Real-time API consumption with loading states

**Development Experience:**
- `vue3-springboot-demo/vite.config.ts` - Proxy configuration for seamless frontend-backend communication
- `springboot3-demo/pom.xml` - Modern Java 17 support with Spring Boot 3 features

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> Loading: User Action
    Loading --> Success: API Response
    Loading --> Error: API Failure
    Success --> Idle: Reset
    Error --> Idle: Retry
    Success --> Loading: Refresh
```

**Development Workflow States:**
- **Development Mode**: Hot reloading with Vite and Spring Boot DevTools
- **Testing Mode**: Unit tests with Vitest and JUnit 5
- **Production Mode**: Optimized builds with minified assets

**API Response States:**
- **Loading State**: Visual feedback during API requests
- **Success State**: Data rendering with proper formatting
- **Error State**: User-friendly error messages and retry mechanisms

---

**Sources:**
- [DemoApplication.java](springboot3-demo/src/main/java/com/example/demo/DemoApplication.java)
- [HelloController.java](springboot3-demo/src/main/java/com/example/demo/controller/HelloController.java)
- [application.yml](springboot3-demo/src/main/resources/application.yml)
- [pom.xml](springboot3-demo/pom.xml)
- [package.json](vue3-springboot-demo/package.json)
- [vite.config.ts](vue3-springboot-demo/vite.config.ts)
- [App.vue](vue3-springboot-demo/src/App.vue)
- [ApiDemoView.vue](vue3-springboot-demo/src/views/ApiDemoView.vue)
- [api/index.ts](vue3-springboot-demo/src/api/index.ts)
- [router/index.ts](vue3-springboot-demo/src/router/index.ts)
- [DemoApplicationTests.java](springboot3-demo/src/test/java/com/example/demo/DemoApplicationTests.java)

</blog>
